series_title: System A+W World Variations on Sensory-Novelty Fixed Agent
series_description: A world-only series for System A+W with the exp_08 sensory-novelty-dominant agent held fixed. The series probes how topology, regeneration structure, scarcity, and map scale reshape behavior when only the environment changes.

experiments.label: world-baseline-anchor
experiments.title: Baseline World Anchor
hypothesis: This run should reproduce the characteristic sensory-novelty behavior of exp_08.
It should serve as the comparison point for every later world variation.
Coverage and revisit behavior should sit near the already observed exp_08 pattern.

experiments.label: world-open-fast-regen
experiments.title: Open World with Fast Uniform Regeneration
hypothesis: Sensory novelty should stay high because many cells continue changing.
Revisit behavior may increase because previously harvested areas recover quickly.
Survival should improve if the agent can exploit recurring local change.

experiments.label: world-sparse-scarcity
experiments.title: Sparse Scarcity Patches
hypothesis: Coverage pressure should rise because rewards are spatially sparse.
Survival may worsen if sensory novelty keeps the agent moving away from productive cells.
Successful exploitation should depend more on finding the right sparse patches.

experiments.label: world-rich-cluster-islands
experiments.title: Rich Cluster Islands
hypothesis: Revisit behavior should increase around the richest changing islands.
Resource gain may improve if sensory novelty tracks cluster recovery well.
Coverage may shrink because fewer regions remain behaviorally attractive.

experiments.label: world-toroidal-wrap
experiments.title: Toroidal Wraparound Grid
hypothesis: Edge-avoidance artifacts should disappear.
Coverage may increase because traversal paths become less interrupted.
Sensory novelty may stay more spatially continuous across former borders.

experiments.label: world-large-open-field
experiments.title: Larger Open Field
hypothesis: Unique cells visited should increase in absolute terms but may fall proportionally.
The agent may struggle to repeatedly exploit good regions in a larger search space.
Survival may drop if exploration costs outrun rediscovery of resources.

experiments.label: world-compact-dense-feedback
experiments.title: Compact Dense-Feedback Arena
hypothesis: Revisit behavior should increase because the agent cycles through the same cells more frequently.
Sensory novelty may remain high despite lower total coverage.
Survival should improve if the tighter world keeps resources within easier reach.

experiments.label: world-long-cooldown
experiments.title: Long Cooldown Regeneration
hypothesis: Sensory novelty near harvested regions should collapse for longer periods.
The agent may be forced into wider exploration before local recovery becomes visible again.
Resource efficiency may worsen if the agent repeatedly checks unrecovered areas.
